_meta.name: okta-revoke-app-from-user
_meta.description: Revoke (unassign) an application from an Okta user. Navigates to the user's Applications tab, clicks the delete (X) button for the target app row, confirms the 'Unassign Application' dialog, and verifies removal.
_meta.author: session-2026-04-16
_meta.warning: Destructive action — removes app access. User will lose access to the app immediately.

auth.note: Okta OAuth PKCE — session must already be active.

userId.description: Okta user ID (format: 00u{alphanumeric}698). Get from knownUserIds below.

appInstanceId.description: Okta app instance ID (format: 0oa{alphanumeric}698). Get from knownAppIds below.

anomalies: DOM: App row selector is tr[data-se='{appInstanceId}'] — each row is keyed by app instance ID
DOM: Delete button is a.app-user-delete-link (icon-only X button) in .app-actions — NOT a text link
DOM: Confirmation modal title is 'Unassign Application' with input[type='submit'][value='OK'] to confirm
DOM: Confirmation OK is input[type='submit'][value='OK'] — NOT a text link, use selector not click_text
Only directly-assigned apps appear in the app table with a delete button — group-assigned apps cannot be removed here
Apps assigned via group show group name in .app-assignment-type, not 'Individual'

chain.note: Click Applications tab on user profile
Confirm Applications tab loaded. Target app should appear in the assigned apps table with delete button.
Click the X (delete) button in the target app's row. Selector uses appInstanceId from tr[data-se] attribute.
Wait for Unassign Application confirmation modal
Confirm 'Unassign Application' modal is visible with OK and Cancel buttons.
Confirm unassignment. Button is input[type='submit'][value='OK'] — NOT a text link.
Verify app no longer appears in user's Applications tab. Table should not contain a row with appInstanceId.
